name: Ironclad API
what_it_does: Ironclad's API for programmatically launching contract workflows, retrieving contract data, managing signatories, and integrating contract lifecycle management into agent-driven legal operations pipelines.
use_cases: Launching contract review and approval workflows from agent triggers (e.g., CRM deal close)
Retrieving signed contract metadata and extracted clause data for downstream processing
Checking contract status and approval progress in multi-step legal workflows
Creating and updating counterparty records and contract attributes programmatically
Integrating Ironclad contract completion events with ERP or CRM systems via webhooks
not_for: Simple one-off e-signature tasks where DocuSign or HelloSign is sufficient
Small teams or startups — Ironclad is enterprise-priced and enterprise-scoped
Developers without a formal Ironclad enterprise subscription
best_when: Your legal ops team uses Ironclad as the CLM platform and you need agents to automate contract initiation, status tracking, or downstream data extraction from executed agreements.
avoid_when: You only need basic e-signature without full contract lifecycle management, or your budget does not support enterprise CLM pricing.

auth.notes: API tokens generated per Ironclad account for server-side agent use. OAuth2 available for user-context integrations. API tokens are scoped to the workspace. Enterprise SSO is enforced — API credentials are provisioned through the admin console.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No public free tier or sandbox
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise only — pricing by negotiated contract, typically six figures annually
pricing.notes: Ironclad is enterprise CLM software with custom pricing. There is no self-serve or free tier. API access is included with enterprise subscriptions. Requires formal procurement and legal ops evaluation.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise SLA-defined limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API errors include HTTP status and a message field. Validation errors list affected fields. Documentation is thorough for core workflow launch operations but thinner for advanced data extraction endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workflow launches accept an optional externalId for deduplication — agents should always set this to prevent duplicate contracts on retry. Record reads are fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Workflow schemas are defined per Ironclad instance — agents must dynamically fetch the workflow schema before constructing launch payloads
API access requires an active enterprise subscription — no sandbox or developer tier available
Webhook events require a publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint with signature verification
Attribute names are workspace-specific and case-sensitive — mismatches cause silent failures or validation errors
Contract signatories and approval chains can be complex — agents must correctly model multi-party routing or launches will fail

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all endpoints. API tokens are workspace-scoped. OAuth2 available with scope controls for user-context access. Enterprise SSO integration reduces credential sprawl. SOC2 Type II certified.
